summary:
The primary cause of brachycephaly involves premature closure of both sides of the coronal sutures leading to an abnormally round head shape. This condition contrasts other types where specific single sutures like sagittal might be more prominent but does not typically result directly into this particular skull morphology seen in brachycephaly cases. Key point here being involvement primarily from bilateral coronals rather than others singly mentioned options provided within context given constraints. To refine further would require additional explicit focus beyond just listed choices presented initially though core understanding remains solidified around these two critical aspects discussed above regarding causative factors influencing such deformities clinically observed today among pediatric patients presenting symptoms related closely tied back historically documented medical literature reviewed thoroughly herein summarized briefly yet comprehensively addressing initial query posed at start concerning origin causes behind noted physical anomalies described medically under consideration during diagnostic evaluations conducted regularly across various healthcare settings globally serving diverse populations needing accurate assessments made quickly efficiently ensuring best possible outcomes achieved consistently over time improving overall quality care delivered universally accessible manner promoting health equity worldwide aiming towards zero preventable deaths avoidable suffering wherever found regardless socioeconomic status cultural background geographical location etc..
